title: pgx Pooling, Query Contracts, Transactions, and Cancellation · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Query Contract
summary: Handlers create pools, query rows are not closed, transactions use the wrong connection, contexts are replaced, SQL errors lose identity, and shutdown races active work. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go query contract increment in bounded pgx repository and unit-of-work layer. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theo…
objectives: Use parameterized typed queries, explicit columns, row closure, iteration errors, cardinality expectations, and explainable mapping.
Create one bounded pgxpool, verify startup deliberately, observe acquire wait, and close after admission and worker shutdown.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Query Contract · Theory Feed Go Query Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 6775 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove pgx pool, rows, one-connection transactions, cancellation, and error ownership; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
